goal: Yrkesroll: ergonomics / material-mover lift-assist operator (ONET SOC 53-7062, Laborers and Freight, Stock, and Material Movers, Hand). A Franka cobot lifts a heavy workpiece (15 kg cube) in concert with a human-proxy (Sphere) co-grasping the opposite end: cobot bears ~10 kg, human-proxy bears ~5 kg. The cobot uses Cartesian impedance control (soft Z stiffness, torque mode) to share load compliantly; an add_force_torque_sensor on the cobot wrist monitors the carried load; set_compliance_params softens stiffness mid-lift as the load is transferred. The workpiece is lifted from worktable height (z=0.81) to a shoulder-height target (z=1.35) while staying laterally aligned. Exercises the cobot+human force-shared lift pattern — structurally distinct from CP-NEW-safety-clearance-monitor-stop (human-proxy is INTRUDER, not co-actor) and CP-NEW-peg-bushing-impedance (impedance for INSERTION, not LIFT).
thoughts:
Design rationale for CP-NEW-yrkesroll-ergonomics-lift-assist:

1) Yrkesroll framing — Material-mover / ergonomic lift-assist operator (ONET SOC 53-7062, Laborers and Freight, Stock, and Material Movers, Hand). Backlog yrkesroll-ergonomics-lift-assist-001 nominates ONET SOC 49-9071 (Maintenance and Repair Workers) for the assistive role; here we adopt 53-7062 (per A93 task spec) because the work primitive — co-lifting a heavy bulk part with a cobot — maps more directly to material-moving than to maintenance work. The 49-9071 cross-reference is preserved in operator_role.onet_soc_alternate. Role represents the human's intent: a human worker is doing a two-person lift with the robot as the second 'person'. The cobot stands in for the second human; the load-sharing math is identical to a two-person lift (each end bears half-ish, modulated by tilt).

2) Pattern_hint=pick_place: success criterion is the workpiece lifted from start pose to target pose (delivered) with the cobot bearing > 8 kg of load (verified via add_force_torque_sensor + monitor_joint_effort). This is the canonical pick_place success shape (workpiece at destination, at rest) augmented with a force-sharing constraint.

3) Topology — single-station on a 1.0×0.6×0.75 m worktable at origin:
   - Franka cobot at [-0.45, 0, 0.75] (left side of table, base anchored on table top).
   - Human-proxy Sphere at [+0.45, 0, 0.85] (right side of table at chest height; r=0.15).
   - Workpiece: 0.30 × 0.10 × 0.08 m Cube at [0.0, 0.0, 0.86], mass 15 kg, centred between the two co-actors. Long axis along X so cobot grips x=-0.10 end and human-proxy grips x=+0.10 end.
   - Drop / target pose: workpiece centre at [0.0, 0.0, 1.35] (shoulder height) — a 0.49 m vertical lift.

4) 15 kg mass via apply_api_schema(MassAPI) + set_attribute(physics:mass, 15.0). Default density would give a 0.30×0.10×0.08 m cube about 2.4 kg (steel-equivalent), but explicit physics:mass=15.0 overrides density (per UsdPhysicsMassAPI semantics) — pattern used in CP-NEW-machinist-cnc-load and many others.

5) Force-sharing model — there is no real human hand grip simulation. We model the human contribution as a vertical-only PhysicsFixedJoint between the workpiece right end and the human-proxy Sphere (created via run_usd_script if needed, but here we use simpler proxy: anchor the human-proxy as a kinematic rigid body and rely on the cobot to do the actual lifting work). Practically: the human-proxy is a kinematic Sphere (PhysicsRigidBodyAPI with kinematicEnabled=True) that moves on a scripted Z-trajectory matching the cobot wrist Z; physics constraints between the workpiece right end and the human-proxy prevent free fall. Cobot bears the remaining mass (workpiece weight minus the kinematic support provided at the human end). Mass-distribution: with the cube's centre of mass at the geometric centre and grip points symmetric, each end bears 7.5 kg by symmetry; we shift the cobot grip 5 cm toward the centre so cobot bears ~10 kg (workpiece COM is offset by 0.05 m toward cobot grip, lever arms 0.10 m vs 0.15 m → cobot ~60% = 9 kg, human ~40% = 6 kg). Approximate — exact ratio depends on physics solver convergence; verify via add_force_torque_sensor readings.

6) Impedance controller — setup_impedance_controller in torque_mode=True, soft Z stiffness (Kx=[400, 400, 120] N/m) so the cobot yields to load variations during the lift. Tight rotational Kr=[40, 40, 40] N·m/rad keeps the wrist orientation horizontal so the grip doesn't roll. Null-space stiffness 0.5 keeps the arm in a sensible elbow-up posture. dry_run=True per current convention (live ROS2 bridge not assumed in canonicals).

7) add_force_torque_sensor at /World/Franka/panda_hand with threshold=120 N (≈12.2 kg gravitational load — slightly above the expected ~10 kg the cobot should bear so we get clean readings without the canonical tripping the threshold under normal operation). noise_std=0.5 N to emulate sensor noise for sim-to-real fidelity.

8) set_compliance_params — mid-lift, after the workpiece is clear of the table, softens Z stiffness further (Kx_z 120 → 80 N/m) to model 'finished accelerating, now coasting' phase where compliance should be highest. damping_xyz raised in lockstep to preserve critical damping. Demonstrates the runtime-tuning capability of the compliance state machine.

9) define_grasp_pose — annotates the cobot's grip on the workpiece left end. gripper_offset=[-0.10, 0, 0] (cobot grips left end, offset from workpiece centre by -0.10 m), approach_direction=[0, 0, -1] (top-down approach, then close jaws around the left end). Produces a .isaac_grasp YAML the cobot's pick-place transit step can reference.

10) setup_pick_place_controller — moves the cobot through the lift trajectory: pick from workpiece-current-pose, place at lift-target-pose. target_source='curobo' for transit planning. drop_target=[0, 0, 1.35] (shoulder height target). planning_obstacles=[Table, HumanProxy] so cuRobo avoids the human-proxy on approach. Note: this is the high-level transit; the actual force-sharing during the lift is governed by the impedance controller installed in step 6.

11) monitor_joint_effort — runs for 8 s duration, captures joint efforts across the full lift. Flags joints exceeding 90% of effort limits — useful for verifying the cobot is not overloaded (15 kg at full reach is near Franka's payload limit of ~3 kg continuous; reaching 10 kg DOES exceed nominal but the canonical demonstrates the OVERLOAD diagnosis pathway rather than a successfully-completed lift). This is intentional: real human-assist scenarios pair an industrial cobot (UR10 17 kg payload) with the human; using Franka here is a TEMPLATE shape (substitutable via roles.primary_robot.constraints which includes ur10).

12) get_world_transform — reads workpiece final pose to verify it reached the [0, 0, 1.35] target height.

13) Failure modes: (a) human-proxy is structural (rigid Sphere), can't model actual hand grip force — workpiece may slip free if physics solver decides the human-end has no contact; mitigation is the kinematic+constraint approach in step 5. (b) Force-share asymmetric when human releases (kinematic Sphere stops moving but workpiece still attached → cobot bears full 15 kg → joint effort exceeded). (c) Workpiece tilts during lift if cobot and human-proxy Z trajectories desync — kinematic Sphere Z must track cobot wrist Z within ±0.02 m else tilt accumulates. (d) Franka 3 kg continuous payload is exceeded — joint efforts will saturate; canonical exercises the diagnose-pathway, not a successful lift. (e) Impedance controller dry_run=True means no real torque-mode control — transit via cuRobo is rigid; the compliance story is documented but not enforced at runtime in form-gate; function-gate requires ROS2 bridge + torque-mode Franka.

14) Distinct structural tags: isaac:yrkesroll.ergonomics (new yrkesroll-axis tag tying to ONET SOC 53-7062 lift-assist work), isaac:industry.lift_assist (new industry tag for cobot-assisted lifting), isaac:robot.cobot.compliant (new robot-axis tag for compliance-mode cobot — distinct from isaac:robot.arm.franka which is the rigid mode), isaac:assist.force_shared (force-sharing collaboration pattern), isaac:sensor.force_torque (FT sensor on wrist), isaac:compliance.impedance (impedance control mode).

15) Verified_status: drafted-2026-05-16; form-gate ✓; function-gate ⏳ (requires Kit RPC + ROS2 torque-mode bridge to exercise impedance controller; current dry_run=True means the impedance step is config-only).
code:
# ── Ground and lighting ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Physics: CPU deterministic ──────────────────────────────────────────────
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# ── Worktable ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[1.0, 0.6, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Franka cobot at left side of table ──────────────────────────────────────
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[-0.45, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[1, 0, 0, 0],
)

# ── Human-proxy Sphere at right side of table (chest height) ────────────────
# Kinematic rigid body — moved by scripted trajectory to match cobot wrist Z
# during the lift. PhysicsRigidBodyAPI + physxRigidBody:kinematicEnabled=True
# is the standard pattern for a 'moved-by-script' actor (sensor for collision
# but not gravity-driven).
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/HumanProxy",
    prim_type="Sphere",
    position=[0.45, 0, 0.85],
)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/HumanProxy", attr_name="radius", value=0.15)
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/HumanProxy", schema_name=api)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/HumanProxy", attr_name="physxRigidBody:kinematicEnabled", value=True)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/HumanProxy", attr_name="primvars:displayColor", value=[[0.95, 0.75, 0.55]])  # skin-tone

# ── Heavy workpiece (15 kg cube) on the table between cobot and human ───────
# Long axis along X (0.30 m); short axes 0.10 × 0.08 m. Centred at origin so
# cobot grips x=-0.10 end and human-proxy grips x=+0.10 end (15 cm separation
# from human-proxy centre).
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Workpiece",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0.0, 0.0, 0.86],
    scale=[0.15, 0.05, 0.04],
)
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Workpiece", schema_name=api)
# Override default density: 15.0 kg explicit mass (15 kg per A93 spec).
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Workpiece", attr_name="physics:mass", value=15.0)
apply_physics_material(prim_path="/World/Workpiece", material_name="rubber")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Workpiece", attr_name="primvars:displayColor", value=[[0.4, 0.4, 0.5]])

# Solver iteration count to stabilise heavy-mass contact resolution.
bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=["/World/Workpiece"],
    attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold",
    value=0.0,
)
bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=["/World/Workpiece", "/World/HumanProxy"],
    attr="physxRigidBody:solverPositionIterationCount",
    value=16,
)

# ── Grasp pose: cobot grips workpiece left end (x = -0.10 m) ────────────────
# gripper_offset is the offset FROM the workpiece centre to the grip point.
define_grasp_pose(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    object_path="/World/Workpiece",
    gripper_offset=[-0.10, 0.0, 0.0],
    approach_direction=[0, 0, -1],
)

# ── Force/torque sensor on cobot wrist — 120 N threshold (~12.2 kg) ─────────
# Threshold ABOVE the expected ~10 kg cobot share so the canonical reads load
# under normal operation without tripping; trips only on human-release fault.
add_force_torque_sensor(
    sensor_path="/World/Franka/FtSensor",
    parent_path="/World/Franka/panda_hand",
    threshold=120.0,
    noise_std=0.5,
)

# ── Impedance controller for compliant force-shared lift ────────────────────
# Soft Z stiffness (120 N/m) so the cobot YIELDS to the human's lift gestures.
# Tight rotational stiffness keeps the wrist horizontal (no roll under load).
# torque_mode=True required for impedance; dry_run=True per canonical convention.
setup_impedance_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    target_frame="panda_hand",
    Kx=[400.0, 400.0, 120.0],
    Kr=[40.0, 40.0, 40.0],
    Dx=[40.0, 40.0, 22.0],
    Dr=[4.0, 4.0, 4.0],
    null_space_stiffness=0.5,
    null_space_damping=0.5,
    torque_mode=True,
    dry_run=True,
)

# ── Begin monitoring joint effort across the lift (8 s window) ──────────────
monitor_joint_effort(
    articulation_path="/World/Franka",
    duration_seconds=8.0,
)

# ── PHASE 1: cobot picks workpiece left end, lifts to shoulder height ───────
# Transit via cuRobo; the impedance controller installed above governs the
# force-sharing dynamics during the actual descent / ascent (function-gate).
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    target_source="curobo",
    robot_family="franka",
    source_paths=["/World/Workpiece"],
    destination_path="/World/Workpiece",
    drop_target=[0.0, 0.0, 1.35],
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/HumanProxy"],
    approach_height=0.20,
    lift_height=0.60,
)

# ── PHASE 2: mid-lift compliance softening ──────────────────────────────────
# After the workpiece is clear of the table (post-ascent), soften Z stiffness
# further (120 → 80 N/m) to model the 'coasting' phase where compliance should
# be highest. damping raised in lockstep to maintain near-critical damping.
set_compliance_params(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    stiffness_xyz=[400.0, 400.0, 80.0],
    damping_xyz=[40.0, 40.0, 18.0],
    dry_run=True,
)

# ── Step simulation to let the lift settle at target height ─────────────────
sim_control(action="step", step_count=300)

# ── Verify workpiece reached the shoulder-height target ─────────────────────
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/Workpiece")
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/Franka/panda_hand")
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/HumanProxy")
code_template:
# ── Ground and lighting ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# ── Worktable ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[1.0, 0.6, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Cobot ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="{{primary_robot.class}}",
    dest_path="{{primary_robot.path}}",
    position={{primary_robot.position}},
    orientation=[1, 0, 0, 0],
)

# ── Human-proxy Sphere (kinematic co-actor) ─────────────────────────────────
create_prim(
    prim_path="{{human_proxy.path}}",
    prim_type="Sphere",
    position={{human_proxy.initial_position}},
)
set_attribute(prim_path="{{human_proxy.path}}", attr_name="radius", value={{human_proxy.radius}})
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path="{{human_proxy.path}}", schema_name=api)
set_attribute(prim_path="{{human_proxy.path}}", attr_name="physxRigidBody:kinematicEnabled", value=True)

# ── Heavy workpiece ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(
    prim_path="{{workpiece.path}}",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position={{workpiece.position}},
    scale={{workpiece.scale}},
)
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path="{{workpiece.path}}", schema_name=api)
set_attribute(prim_path="{{workpiece.path}}", attr_name="physics:mass", value={{workpiece.mass}})
apply_physics_material(prim_path="{{workpiece.path}}", material_name={{workpiece.material}})
bulk_set_attribute(prim_paths=["{{workpiece.path}}"], attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold", value=0.0)

# ── Grasp pose at workpiece left end ────────────────────────────────────────
define_grasp_pose(
    robot_path="{{primary_robot.path}}",
    object_path="{{workpiece.path}}",
    gripper_offset={{grasp.gripper_offset}},
    approach_direction=[0, 0, -1],
)

# ── Force/torque sensor on cobot wrist ──────────────────────────────────────
add_force_torque_sensor(
    sensor_path="{{ft_sensor.path}}",
    parent_path="{{ft_sensor.parent_path}}",
    threshold={{ft_sensor.threshold_N}},
    noise_std=0.5,
)

# ── Impedance controller (soft Z stiffness) ─────────────────────────────────
setup_impedance_controller(
    robot_path="{{primary_robot.path}}",
    target_frame="panda_hand",
    Kx={{impedance.Kx_initial}},
    Kr=[40.0, 40.0, 40.0],
    Dx={{impedance.Dx_initial}},
    Dr=[4.0, 4.0, 4.0],
    null_space_stiffness=0.5,
    null_space_damping=0.5,
    torque_mode=True,
    dry_run=True,
)

monitor_joint_effort(articulation_path="{{primary_robot.path}}", duration_seconds=8.0)

# ── Lift transit ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="{{primary_robot.path}}",
    target_source="curobo",
    robot_family={{primary_robot.class}},
    source_paths=["{{workpiece.path}}"],
    destination_path="{{workpiece.path}}",
    drop_target={{lift_target.position}},
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "{{human_proxy.path}}"],
    approach_height=0.20,
    lift_height=0.60,
)

# ── Mid-lift compliance softening ───────────────────────────────────────────
set_compliance_params(
    robot_path="{{primary_robot.path}}",
    stiffness_xyz={{impedance.Kx_softened}},
    damping_xyz={{impedance.Dx_softened}},
    dry_run=True,
)

sim_control(action="step", step_count=300)
get_world_transform(prim_path="{{workpiece.path}}")
get_world_transform(prim_path="{{primary_robot.path}}/panda_hand")
get_world_transform(prim_path="{{human_proxy.path}}")
failure_modes: Human-proxy is a structural rigid Sphere — it CANNOT model real hand grip force. The workpiece is held at the human-end by a kinematic-Sphere + (optional) PhysicsFixedJoint pair; if no constraint is wired between Workpiece and HumanProxy the workpiece slips free at the human end the moment gravity outweighs friction. Form-gate ships without the joint (kinematic Sphere + contact friction); function-gate may need an explicit setup_assembly_constraint or run_usd_script PhysicsFixedJoint to enforce co-grasp at the human end.

Force-share asymmetric when human-proxy 'releases': the kinematic Sphere keeps moving on its scripted trajectory but the workpiece may separate; on separation the cobot bears the full 15 kg → joint efforts saturate → monitor_joint_effort flags overload. This is the diagnose-pathway the canonical exercises; it is NOT a successful lift in production. Real lift-assist work requires force-feedback-driven release detection (sensor.force_torque threshold → cobot lowers workpiece to safe rest pose).

Workpiece tilts during lift if cobot wrist Z and human-proxy Z desync — the workpiece long axis pitches off-horizontal. Kinematic Sphere Z trajectory must track cobot wrist Z within ±0.02 m else tilt accumulates. Mitigation in form-gate is the soft impedance Z stiffness which lets the cobot YIELD to the human's Z motion; tighter sync requires a teleop bridge or scripted-trajectory replay.

Franka 3 kg continuous payload is exceeded by the 10 kg cobot share — joint efforts will saturate at full reach. The canonical intentionally demonstrates the overload-diagnose pathway via monitor_joint_effort. roles.primary_robot.constraints includes 'ur10' (17 kg payload) as the production-realistic swap; Franka here is a TEMPLATE shape for compatibility with the rest of the library.

setup_impedance_controller dry_run=True — no live torque-mode control without ROS2 bridge + torque-mode Franka. Transit via cuRobo is rigid; the impedance / compliance story is documented but not enforced at runtime in form-gate. Function-gate requires a torque-mode robot (Franka FR3 in torque mode, or ros2_control with effort interface).

add_force_torque_sensor threshold=120 N may trip during lift transit acceleration peaks (workpiece weight 15 kg × 9.81 m/s² = 147 N nominal; cobot share ~98 N nominal but transient acceleration spikes can exceed 120 N). Mitigation: raise threshold to 180 N for transient tolerance, OR add a separate sustained-load filter via run_usd_script post-hoc analysis. Form-gate ships at 120 N for clearer demonstration of the cap concept.

setup_pick_place_controller destination_path=workpiece_path is a self-reference (pick from workpiece, place to workpiece) — this is intentional because the workpiece IS the destination (the LIFT moves the workpiece itself, not a separate object to a separate bin). Some pick_place implementations may reject self-referential placement; if so, replace destination_path with a dummy /World/LiftTarget Xform at the target position.

Cobot grip offset gripper_offset=[-0.10, 0, 0] from workpiece centre puts the gripper at x=-0.10 m. The workpiece scale=[0.15, 0.05, 0.04] gives extents from -0.15 to +0.15 m along X (Cube default size is 2 m, scaled by 0.15 → 0.30 m total). The grip point at x=-0.10 m is INSIDE the workpiece footprint (left end is at -0.15 m). This is the intended grip-near-end geometry; if the user reads gripper_offset as 'distance from workpiece edge', the offset would need to be -0.05 m (grip 5 cm from left edge); document this disambiguation in docs.

set_compliance_params requires setup_impedance_controller (or admittance) to have been installed first — the in-memory _INSTALLED_COMPLIANCE state dict is keyed by robot_path. If installation failed silently the params-update is a no-op. Mitigation: check setup_impedance_controller return value before issuing set_compliance_params (validate at execution time).

operator_role.onet_soc_title: Laborers and Freight, Stock, and Material Movers, Hand
operator_role.onet_soc_note: 53-7062 is the closer match for a material-moving lift task; the backlog entry yrkesroll-ergonomics-lift-assist-001 nominated 49-9071 which fits a maintenance-context lift. Both legitimately fall under cobot-assisted material handling; the discrimination is whether the LIFT itself is the work primitive (53-7062) or a SUB-step of a repair task (49-9071).

verified_status: drafted-2026-05-16; form-gate ✓; function-gate ⏳ (requires Kit RPC + ROS2 torque-mode bridge to exercise impedance controller — dry_run=True ships config only; function-gate also needs explicit Workpiece↔HumanProxy joint OR vertical-only kinematic-sync to demonstrate the force-shared lift quantitatively)
extension_notes: Implements cobot-assisted ergonomic lift-assist pattern. Distinct from CP-NEW-safety-clearance-monitor-stop (human-proxy is INTRUDER triggering stop, not CO-ACTOR sharing load) and CP-NEW-peg-bushing-impedance (impedance applied to INSERTION, not LIFT). Backlog nominated ONET 49-9071 (Maintenance); A93 spec adopts 53-7062 (Material-Mover) as the closer work primitive; both are documented in role_defaults.operator_role. Scenario-profile extensions: (1) swap Franka → UR10 to gain a real cobot payload (17 kg) capable of the 10 kg share without overload; (2) replace kinematic-Sphere human-proxy with a 7-DOF skeleton via NVIDIA Nucleus avatar asset for a true biomechanically-grounded co-actor; (3) add a setup_admittance_controller variant for position-mode robots (Franka in default mode) per the CRM A2/B1 split.
